zh.Le Thour, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, F: 法國哈帕格-亞登阿爾登斯勒 爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Raoul II DU THOUR於大約1150年出生在法國哈帕格-亞登阿爾登斯勒 爾時，他的父親Raoul Ier DU THOUR22歲，母親Alix DE CURIS17歲。他和Alice DE RUMIGNY至少有1個女兒。

zh-hans.Le Thour, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, F: 法国哈帕格-亞登阿爾登斯勒 爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Raoul II DU THOUR于大约1150年出生在法国哈帕格-亞登阿爾登斯勒 爾时，他的父亲Raoul Ier DU THOUR22岁，母亲Alix DE CURIS17岁。他和Alice DE RUMIGNY至少有1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: DU
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: THOUR
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Erland, Eyvind, Nels.  Southeast Asian Chor, Tong, Chia, Chu, Kong, Seng, Tou, Chee, Ching, Chou, Chue, Eng, Thai, Moua, Neng, Pao, Yeng, Yer, Blong, Chong, Choua, Houa, Shoua, Soua.
Swedish and Danish: from a short form of any of various Scandinavian personal names containing the first element Thor (Old Norse Thórr), the name of the god of thunder in Scandinavian mythology.
English: from the Anglo-Scandinavian name Thōr, Thūr, probably short forms of Old Norse compound names in Thór-, Thúr- (see 1 above).

variants.name: Thorsen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Thor, Niels, Lars, Anders, Arnt, Erik, Iver, Jarl, Knute, Magnar, Oystein, Viggo.
Danish and Norwegian: patronymic from the personal name   Thor  .

variants.name: Thoren
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Hilmer, Lennart.
Swedish (Thorén): ornamental name composed of the personal name Thor + the adjectival suffix -én, a derivative of Latin -enius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Thoresen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Birgit, Borge, Britt, Lars, Nils, Paal, Thoralf.
Danish and Norwegian: patronymic from the personal name   Thor  .

variants.name: Thorsell
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Anders, Erik.

Swedish: ornamental name from the personal name Thor + possessive -s- + the suffix -ell, a derivative of the Latin adjectival ending -elius. Alternatively, the element Thors may represent a placename formed with Tors-, as for example Torshälla.

variants.name: Portmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Helmut, Franz, Hansi, Kurt.

North German: occupational name for a gatekeeper, from Middle Low German port(e) ‘gate’ (from Latin porta) + man ‘man’, or a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a fortified town. Compare Porter .

variants.name: Thorell
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Lennart, Nils, Algot, Bjorn, Erik.
Swedish: ornamental name from the personal name Thor + the suffix -ell, a derivative of the Latin adjectival ending -elius. Compare Torell .

variants.name: Tharaldson
meanings: Americanized form of Norwegian Tharaldsen: patronymic from the personal name Tharald, from Old Norse Thór(v)aldr, composed of the name of the god Thórr (see Thor ) + valdr ‘ruler’.

variants.name: Tellefsen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Tellef, Aane, Ingeborg, Toralf, Torleif.
Norwegian: patronymic from Tellef, a form of the Old Norse personal name Thorleifr, composed of the name of the god Thórr (see Thor ) + leifr ‘heir, descendant’.

variants.name: Thore
meanings: English (Staffordshire and Middlesex): chiefly from the Middle English male personal name T(h)or(e), Thure, representing Old Norse Thórir or Thóri, or Anglo-Scandinavian Thórr, all short forms of Old Norse personal names containing the first element Thór-, Thúr- ‘Thor’, the name of the god of thunder in Scandinavian mythology. Occasionally perhaps also from the corresponding Middle English female personal name Thore (Old Norse Thóra). This surname is very rare in Britain and Ireland.
Swedish: variant of   Thor  .
French (Thoré): nickname for a strong or violent individual, derived from Old French tor ‘bull’.

variants.name: Thorburn
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scottish Iain.
English: from a northern Middle English personal name T(h)orbern, T(h)urbern, Old Norse Thórbjörn, composed of the elements Thórr (see Thor ) + björn ‘bear, warrior’. Compare Durban .

nameMeanings.name: Raoul
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Germanic. See Ralph .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  ♂ ‘Wondrous’ or ‘enlightenment’.
2  ♀ ‘Fine jade’.

en.lifeSummary: When Raoul II DU THOUR was born about 1150, in Le Thour, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, France, his father, Raoul Ier DU THOUR, was 22 and his mother, Alix DE CURIS, was 17. He had at least 1 daughter with Alice DE RUMIGNY.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Raoul II DU THOUR est né environ 1150, à Le Thour, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, France, son père, Raoul Ier DU THOUR, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Alix DE CURIS, avait 17 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Alice DE RUMIGNY.

de.lifeSummary: Als Raoul II DU THOUR um 1150 in Le Thour, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Raoul Ier DU THOUR 22 und seine Mutter Alix DE CURIS 17. Er hatte mit Alice DE RUMIGNY mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Raoul II DU THOUR è nato il circa 1150 nel Le Thour, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia, il suo padre, Raoul Ier DU THOUR, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Alix DE CURIS, aveva 17 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Alice DE RUMIGNY.

ja.Le Thour, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, F: フランスチャンプアグネ-アルデンアルデンスル ソウル
ja.lifeSummary: Raoul II DU THOURが約1150年にフランスチャンプアグネ-アルデンアルデンスル ソウルで生まれた時，父Raoul Ier DU THOURは22歳，母Alix DE CURISは17歳であった。彼はAlice DE RUMIGNYとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Raoul II DU THOUR(이)가 대략 1150년 프랑스 챔파그니-아르데니 아데네스 일 토우르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Raoul Ier DU THOUR(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Alix DE CURIS(은)는 17세였다. 그는 Alice DE RUMIGNY(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Raoul II DU THOUR nasceu aproximadamente 1150, em Le Thour, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, França, seu pai, Raoul Ier DU THOUR, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Alix DE CURIS, tinha 17 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Alice DE RUMIGNY.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Raoul II DU THOUR родился около 1150 г. в местности л тоур, арденнесь, чампагне-арденн, Франция, его отцу по имени Raoul Ier DU THOUR было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Alix DE CURIS, 17. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Alice DE RUMIGNY.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Raoul II DU THOUR nació aproximadamente 1150, en Le Thour, Ardennes, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia, su papá, Raoul Ier DU THOUR, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Alix DE CURIS, tenía 17 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Alice DE RUMIGNY.
